message: fix(repair): fail closed on temp nonce exhaustion (bd-s6t51)

files.patch:
@@ -1189,6 +1189,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-s35v4","title":"add FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX (v2) ioctl support","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse implements FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY (v1 policy, 0x400C6615) but not the newer FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX (0xC0166616) which supports v2 policies with additional fields (contents_encryption_mode, filenames_encryption_mode, master_key_identifier).

Scope:
- Add FsOps::get_encryption_policy_ex() trait method with default fallback
- Define FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX constant and fscrypt_get_policy_ex_arg struct
- Parse on-disk fscrypt_context_v2 from xattr if present
- Wire dispatch in ffs-fuse ioctl handler with size/version validation
- Add unit dispatch tests for v1-only, v2, and unencrypted cases
- Add mounted-path E2E test on encrypted ext4 image if available

Acceptance:
- FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX returns v1 or v2 policy depending on on-disk format
- Direct dispatch tests verify struct encoding and version negotiation
- Unencrypted files return ENODATA cleanly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:10.609876351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:45:58.763206138Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:45:58.760892339Z","close_reason":"FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX (v2) already implemented at crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1866-1907 with size validation (ERANGE when out_size < header + actual_policy_size), v1/v2 version negotiation, and 5 passing dispatch tests at crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:5323-5499 (v1 encoding, v2 encoding, short-v1-buffer rejection, v1-sized-for-v2 regression bd-3yy9k, ENODATA propagation). FsOps::get_encryption_policy_ex routed through backend. Stale >48h; closing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-s4eea","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for create mkdir link and symlink","description":"Survey finding: ext4 fuse_e2e proves create, mkdir, hard-link, and symlink success on writable mounts, while ffs-fuse already hard-rejects these mutations with EROFS on read-only mounts. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that attempt create, mkdir, hard_link, and symlink on a read-only mount and assert exact EROFS with unchanged directory contents. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes read-only operator-visible refusal for these entry-creation mutations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:25:48.819671875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-s4tww","title":"Freeze exact MountHandle and FuseInner debug contracts","description":"Replace substring-only debug assertions in ffs-fuse with exact golden contracts for MountHandle, FuseInner, and CacheLinePadded debug output; verify via focused rch tests plus workspace fmt/check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T04:12:08.450829259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T04:19:38.715661697Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T04:19:38.713521909Z","close_reason":"Added exact golden debug contracts for MountHandle, FuseInner, and CacheLinePadded; verified with focused rch tests plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["golden","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-s6t51","title":"Repair ownership temp record nonce can wrap and reuse paths","description":"Repair ownership atomic writes derive temp record paths from a process id plus TEMP_FILE_COUNTER.fetch_add. When the global counter reaches u64::MAX, fetch_add wraps to zero and can reuse earlier temp paths, weakening the write-to-temp + rename protocol. Convert nonce allocation to fail closed at exhaustion and add numeric-limit coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:19:47.820709338Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:24:09.853400193Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T11:24:09.851205646Z","close_reason":"Made repair ownership temp record nonce allocation fail closed at numeric exhaustion and added coverage proving it does not wrap.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-sb8p2","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] apply_fcw_commit silently falls back to bytes.clone() on merge-bytes None, risking lost updates if preflight/apply diverge","description":"# Review finding (review-only pass on 2026-04-23)

## Location

`crates/ffs-mvcc/src/lib.rs` lines ~2095-2102 inside `apply_fcw_commit`, and the mirrored block ~2182-2189 inside `commit_ssi_internal`.

## Finding

When `self.latest_commit_seq(block) > snapshot.high`, the apply path re-runs the merge:

```rust
proof.merge_bytes(&base, &latest, &bytes)
    .unwrap_or_else(|| {
        tracing::error!("preflight_fcw missed an unmergeable conflict on block {block:?}");
        bytes.clone()
    })
```

If `merge_bytes` returns `None`, the fallback silently keeps our staged `bytes`, overwriting the concurrent writer's version. That is a classic lost-update; the only signal is a `tracing::error!` line.

## Why this is currently unreachable (and why it still matters)

`preflight_fcw` calls `preflight_resolve_block_conflict`, which routes through `resolved_write_bytes` and returns `CommitError::Conflict` on a None merge. Because `commit_fcw_internal` runs preflight then apply under the same `&mut self`, no other writer can mutate `versions` in between, so the inputs to `merge_bytes` are identical, so the outputs match. The fallback is defensive code for an invariant that currently holds.

The risk is future refactors: if anyone (a) separates preflight and apply without holding the MvccStore write lock across them (the `commit_fcw_prechecked` contract is 'should hold', not enforced), or (b) introduces GC/compaction between preflight and apply, or (c) changes how `version_bytes_at` resolves, the fallback silently corrupts data rather than aborting the commit.

## Suggested fix (small)

Replace `unwrap_or_else` with `debug_assert!(false, ...)` followed by the fallback, OR hoist the merge resolution earlier (before the `let Transaction { .. } = txn` destructure) so the commit can abort with `CommitError::Conflict` on merge failure — matching the `resolved_writes_for_commit` pre-journal flow already used by `commit_transaction_journaled` in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:4347`.

## Acceptance

- apply_fcw_commit and commit_ssi_internal abort (or panic in debug) on merge_bytes None rather than silently overwriting.
- Regression test: inject a transaction whose merge proof refuses at apply time (e.g., MergeProof::Unsafe with a real conflict passed into the internal apply stage via test hook) and assert the commit returns `CommitError::Conflict`.
- Existing commit/merge tests still pass.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] — latent data-loss risk. Not currently reachable under the documented locking contract, but the fallback is the kind of silent defensive branch that hides invariant violations rather than surfacing them.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:40:09.432555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:20:54.886557581Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:20:54.884613517Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by 90d8334; verified with rch ffs-mvcc prechecked conflict regression","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
